meta.description: Ordered list of remediation tasks for an LLM agent. Each task is self-contained with enough context to implement the fix without re-reading the audit. Tasks should be executed in the order given; later tasks may depend on earlier ones.
execution_notes: After each task, run: uv run ruff check . && uv run pytest
For schema tasks, also run the migration SQL against a test copy of project_manager.db before applying to production.
Do not merge tasks across different files unless explicitly noted as atomic.

tasks.title: Fix entry point: add main(), correct module typo, call init_db()
tasks.rationale: uv run AccessMan will fail with ModuleNotFoundError (typo: accessibility_mrg vs accessibility_mgr) and AttributeError (no main() function). Even if those were fixed, init_db() is only in __main__ guard, so the DB is never created on console-script launch.

changes.insert:
def main() -> None:
    """Console-script entry point for `uv run AccessMan`."""
    DB.init_db()
    MainApp().run()

changes.old:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    DB.init_db()
    app = MainApp()
    app.run()
changes.new:
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

changes.old: accessibility-manager = "accessibility_mrg.app:main"
changes.new: AccessMan = "accessibility_mgr.app:main"

verification: uv run AccessMan
App window opens without errors
project_manager.db and prints_files/ are created on first launch

tasks.title: Fix silent data corruption in braille job and LP job add callbacks
tasks.rationale: After inserting a new braille/LP job, the code re-fetches all jobs and updates jobs[0], assuming it is the newly inserted row. This is fragile and can write step data to the wrong record.

changes.new:
def add_braille_job(title: str, braille_type: str, notes: str = "") -> int:
    """Insert a braille job and return the new row id."""
    with get_conn() as c:
        cur = c.execute(
            "INSERT INTO braille_job (title,braille_type,notes) VALUES (?,?,?)",
            (title, braille_type, notes)
        )
        return cur.lastrowid

def add_lp_job(title: str, job_type: str, notes: str = "") -> int:
    """Insert an LP/eBraille job and return the new row id."""
    with get_conn() as c:
        cur = c.execute(
            "INSERT INTO lp_ebraille_job (title,job_type,notes) VALUES (?,?,?)",
            (title, job_type, notes)
        )
        return cur.lastrowid

verification: Add a new braille job with all 5 steps checked
Verify the correct row in the DB has all steps set to 1
Add two braille jobs in rapid succession and verify step data is on the correct rows

tasks.title: Harden dynamic UPDATE SQL builders against column-name injection
tasks.rationale: All update helpers interpolate field names directly into SQL. The allow-list guards against unknown columns but the code violates STYLE.md and is fragile. Adding an explicit guard makes intent clear and prevents future regressions.

changes.code:
def _build_update_sql(table: str, fields: dict, allowed: set) -> tuple[str, list]:
    """Build a parameterised UPDATE SQL statement.

    Column names are validated against `allowed` to prevent injection.
    Values are always passed as parameters, never interpolated.
    """
    safe = {k: v for k, v in fields.items() if k in allowed}
    if not safe:
        raise ValueError(f"No valid fields to update in table '{table}'")
    sets = ", ".join(f"{k} = ?" for k in safe)
    sql = f"UPDATE {table} SET {sets}, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?"  # noqa: S608
    values = list(safe.values())
    return sql, values

verification: uv run ruff check . reports no issues
Edit a filament record and verify updated_at changes
Pass an unknown field name and verify ValueError is raised

tasks.title: Implement dynamic materials/options system with admin UI
tasks.rationale: User requirement: 'allow me to add items to the database (i.e., type of materials I need and options available) … flexible enough for me to use with newly emerging options I cannot account for yet'. All category lists are currently hard-coded Python constants with no DB backing.

changes.description: Add two new tables and seed data to the existing executescript in init_db()
changes.sql_to_add:
-- ── Material categories (dynamic, user-extendable) ─────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS material_category (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    section     TEXT NOT NULL,
    value       TEXT NOT NULL,
    label       TEXT NOT NULL,
    sort_order  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    active      INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    created_at  TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    UNIQUE(section, value)
);

-- ── Workflow steps (dynamic, user-extendable) ───────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workflow_step (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    job_type    TEXT NOT NULL,
    step_key    TEXT NOT NULL,
    label       TEXT NOT NULL,
    sort_order  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    active      INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    UNIQUE(job_type, step_key)
);

changes.description: Add CRUD helpers for material_category and workflow_step tables

Replace all hard-coded constants (PAPER_TYPES, ELEC_CATS, ELEC_UNITS, BRAILLE_TYPES, LP_TYPES) with DB queries in the on_mount / compose methods of each modal. BRAILLE_STEPS and LP_STEPS should be loaded from workflow_step table. See detailed sub-steps below.

sub_steps.description: Remove the module-level constants PAPER_TYPES, ELEC_CATS, ELEC_UNITS, BRAILLE_TYPES, LP_TYPES. Keep BRAILLE_STEPS and LP_STEPS temporarily as fallbacks but load from DB at runtime.
In PaperModal.compose(), replace the opts variable: opts = [(r['label'], r['value']) for r in DB.list_material_categories('paper_type')]

In ElecModal.compose(), replace ELEC_CATS reference: cats = [(r['label'], r['value']) for r in DB.list_material_categories('elec_cat')]. Replace ELEC_UNITS reference: units = [(r['label'], r['value']) for r in DB.list_material_categories('elec_unit')]

In BrailleJobModal.compose(), replace BRAILLE_TYPES: btypes = [(r['label'], r['value']) for r in DB.list_material_categories('braille_type')]. Replace BRAILLE_STEPS iteration: steps = DB.list_workflow_steps('braille'); iterate over steps to yield Checkbox widgets using r['step_key'] as ID and r['label'] as text.
In LPJobModal.compose(), replace LP_TYPES and LP_STEPS similarly using 'lp_type' and 'lp_ebraille' sections.
In FilamentModal.compose(), replace the hard-coded filament_type Input with a Select widget populated from DB.list_material_categories('filament_type').
Update _progress_bar() to accept a dynamic list of step keys queried from DB.list_workflow_steps(job_type) rather than using the hard-coded BRAILLE_STEPS / LP_STEPS constants.

changes.description: Add a new '⚙ Materials & Steps' sidebar section with a panel class MaterialsAdminPanel. This panel must provide: (1) a section selector (Select or Tab) to choose which category group to manage (paper_type, elec_cat, elec_unit, braille_type, lp_type, filament_type, diameter_mm, braille steps, lp_ebraille steps); (2) a DataTable listing current entries; (3) Add / Deactivate buttons with appropriate modals. Reactivation of soft-deleted entries should also be possible.

tasks.migration_note: Because material_category and workflow_step use INSERT OR IGNORE, running init_db() on an existing database is safe — existing user data is unaffected. However, if the user has existing braille_job or lp_ebraille_job rows with step columns not present in the new workflow_step table, those rows remain valid; the dynamic step loader will simply not render checkboxes for steps not in the DB.
verification: Launch app; all existing Select widgets populate correctly from DB
Add a new paper type via the admin panel; it appears in PaperModal's Select on next open
Add a new workflow step for 'braille'; it appears as a checkbox in BrailleJobModal
Deactivate an option; it no longer appears in the relevant Select
uv run ruff check . reports no issues

tasks.title: Align Python version constraints across pyproject.toml and docs
tasks.rationale: pyproject.toml requires-python = '>=3.11' but classifiers list 3.9/3.10, STYLE.md targets 3.9+, and CONTRIBUTING.md says Python 3.9+. This will silently reject 3.9/3.10 installs.

changes.old: requires-python = ">=3.11"
changes.new: requires-python = ">=3.9"

changes.old: target-version = "py311"
changes.new: target-version = "py39"

changes.note: STYLE.md already says Python 3.9+. No change needed, but verify that no 3.10+ syntax (match/case, X|Y union hints) was introduced in app.py.
CONTRIBUTING.md already says Python 3.9+. No change needed.

verification: uv python install 3.9 && uv sync succeeds
No Python 3.10+ syntax in app.py (no match/case, no X | Y type hints in signatures)

tasks.title: Handle FK constraint error on filament delete gracefully
tasks.rationale: Deleting a filament spool referenced by a print job raises a sqlite3.IntegrityError with no user-facing message. The user sees a crash, not a helpful dialog.

changes.new:
def delete_filament(row_id: int) -> None:
    """Delete a filament record. Raises sqlite3.IntegrityError if print jobs reference it."""
    with get_conn() as c:
        c.execute("DELETE FROM filament WHERE id=?", (row_id,))

changes.description: Wrap the DB.delete_filament call in a try/except sqlite3.IntegrityError and show a MsgModal explaining the constraint.
changes.note: Move the sqlite3 import to the top of app.py as part of L-05 cleanup.

verification: Log a print job using filament row #1
Attempt to delete filament row #1
Confirm MsgModal appears with helpful message instead of a crash

tasks.title: Remove dead code _touch_updated()
tasks.rationale: _touch_updated() is defined but never called. It also contains an unvalidated table-name interpolation that constitutes a SQL injection risk.

tasks.title: Replace wildcard import in db/__init__.py with explicit exports
tasks.rationale: from .queries import * re-exports shutil, Path, get_conn, PRINTS_DIR and private helpers into the DB namespace, polluting the API used throughout app.py.
verification: import accessibility_mgr.db as DB; assert not hasattr(DB, 'shutil')
import accessibility_mgr.db as DB; assert not hasattr(DB, 'Path')
All app.py DB.* calls still resolve correctly

tasks.title: Fix run.sh to use uv instead of pip --break-system-packages
tasks.rationale: Using pip with --break-system-packages on modern Linux distros violates PEP 668 protections and can corrupt OS-managed Python environments.

changes.new_content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Launcher for Accessibility Materials Project Manager
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

# Ensure uv is available
if ! command -v uv &>/dev/null; then
    echo "ERROR: 'uv' not found. Install it from https://docs.astral.sh/uv/"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Starting Accessibility Materials Project Manager..."
exec uv run AccessMan "$@"

verification: chmod +x accessibility_mgr/run.sh && ./accessibility_mgr/run.sh
App launches without any pip invocation

tasks.title: Add error handling around shutil.copy2 in add_print_job()
tasks.rationale: If the file copy fails (permissions, disk full, source deleted), the DB record is still inserted with a file_path pointing to a non-existent file, creating orphaned records.

changes.old:
def add_print_job(printer_id, filament_id, filament_used_g, file_source_path=None,
                  successful=1, failure_reason=None, notes=""):
    file_path = None
    file_name = None
    if file_source_path:
        src = Path(file_source_path)
        dest = PRINTS_DIR / src.name
        if src.exists() and src != dest:
            shutil.copy2(src, dest)
        file_path = str(dest.relative_to(PRINTS_DIR.parent))
        file_name = src.name

    with get_conn() as c:
        c.execute(
            """INSERT INTO print_job
               (printer_id,filament_id,filament_used_g,file_path,file_name,successful,failure_reason,notes)
               VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)""",
            (printer_id, filament_id, filament_used_g, file_path, file_name,
             successful, failure_reason, notes)
        )
    if filament_id and filament_used_g:
        deduct_filament(filament_id, filament_used_g)
changes.new:
def add_print_job(
    printer_id: int,
    filament_id: int | None,
    filament_used_g: float,
    file_source_path: str | None = None,
    successful: int = 1,
    failure_reason: str | None = None,
    notes: str = "",
) -> int:
    """Log a print job, optionally copying an attached file. Returns new row id."""
    file_path: str | None = None
    file_name: str | None = None

    if file_source_path:
        src = Path(file_source_path)
        dest = PRINTS_DIR / src.name
        if src.exists() and src != dest:
            try:
                shutil.copy2(src, dest)
                file_path = str(dest.relative_to(PRINTS_DIR.parent))
                file_name = src.name
            except OSError as exc:
                # Log the error but do not abort the job record.
                # file_path / file_name remain None so no orphaned DB reference is created.
                import warnings
                warnings.warn(
                    f"Could not copy print file '{src}' to '{dest}': {exc}",
                    RuntimeWarning,
                    stacklevel=2,
                )
        elif src == dest:
            file_path = str(dest.relative_to(PRINTS_DIR.parent))
            file_name = src.name

    with get_conn() as c:
        cur = c.execute(
            """INSERT INTO print_job
               (printer_id,filament_id,filament_used_g,file_path,file_name,successful,failure_reason,notes)
               VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)""",
            (printer_id, filament_id, filament_used_g, file_path, file_name,
             successful, failure_reason, notes)
        )
        new_id = cur.lastrowid

    if filament_id and filament_used_g:
        deduct_filament(filament_id, filament_used_g)

    return new_id

verification: Log a print job with a valid file path; file appears in prints_files/
Log a print job with a non-existent file path; job is saved without file_path, no crash

tasks.title: Fix connection leak in get_conn() — use a context manager that closes
tasks.rationale: get_conn() returns a raw connection. The 'with get_conn() as c:' pattern commits/rolls back the transaction but does not close the file descriptor. Over long sessions this accumulates open FDs.

changes.old:
def get_conn() -> sqlite3.Connection:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
    conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
    return conn
changes.new:
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Generator

@contextmanager
def get_conn() -> Generator[sqlite3.Connection, None, None]:
    """Yield an open, configured SQLite connection, then commit and close."""
    conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_PATH)
    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
    conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
    conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
    try:
        yield conn
        conn.commit()
    except Exception:
        conn.rollback()
        raise
    finally:
        conn.close()
changes.note: The contextmanager decorator means 'with get_conn() as c:' now yields the connection AND closes it on exit. Remove any explicit conn.commit() / conn.close() calls in init_db() since the context manager handles them.

changes.description: In init_db(), replace the manual conn = get_conn() / conn.commit() / conn.close() pattern with 'with get_conn() as conn:' since get_conn() is now a context manager.
changes.old:
conn = get_conn()
    cur = conn.cursor()
    ...
    conn.commit()
    conn.close()
changes.new:
with get_conn() as conn:
        conn.executescript("""...""")

verification: Run 100 sequential DB operations; verify no 'too many open files' error
Use lsof | grep project_manager.db to confirm FD count stays at 0 between operations

tasks.title: Change INNER JOIN on printer to LEFT JOIN in list_print_jobs()
tasks.rationale: If a printer row is deleted (e.g., user wipes the seed data), all print jobs referencing that printer become invisible in the UI because the INNER JOIN filters them out.

changes.old: JOIN printer p ON p.id = pj.printer_id
changes.new: LEFT JOIN printer p ON p.id = pj.printer_id

verification: Insert a print job with printer_id=999 (non-existent)
list_print_jobs() returns the row with printer_name as None
UI displays '—' or 'Unknown' for the printer name

tasks.title: Give each modal a unique container id instead of sharing id='dialog'
tasks.rationale: All 8 modals use id='dialog' for their root container. This prevents per-modal CSS customisation and would cause conflicts if two modals were mounted simultaneously.

changes.note: Update APP_CSS to target '#confirm_dialog, #msg_dialog, #filament_dialog, #paper_dialog, #elec_dialog, #printjob_dialog, #braille_dialog, #lp_dialog' with the shared dialog styles, keeping per-modal overrides available.

verification: All modals open and display correctly
uv run ruff check . reports no issues

tasks.title: Add Edit capability to PrintJobPanel
tasks.rationale: PrintJobPanel is the only inventory panel with no Edit button. Users cannot correct mistakes (wrong filament, wrong gram count) without deleting and re-adding.

changes.old:
def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with Horizontal():
            yield Button("+ Log Print", id="add_pj", variant="success")
            yield Button("✕ Delete", id="del_pj", variant="error")
            yield Button("📁 Open Files Folder", id="open_folder")
        yield DataTable(id="pj_table", zebra_stripes=True, cursor_type="row")
changes.new:
def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        with Horizontal():
            yield Button("+ Log Print", id="add_pj", variant="success")
            yield Button("✎ Edit", id="edit_pj")
            yield Button("✕ Delete", id="del_pj", variant="error")
            yield Button("📁 Open Files Folder", id="open_folder")
        yield DataTable(id="pj_table", zebra_stripes=True, cursor_type="row")

changes.code:
def update_print_job(row_id: int, **fields: object) -> None:
    """Update a print job record by id."""
    allowed = {"printer_id", "filament_id", "filament_used_g", "successful", "failure_reason", "notes"}
    sql, vals = _build_update_sql("print_job", fields, allowed)
    # print_job has no updated_at column — override the helper's assumption
    sets = ", ".join(f"{k} = ?" for k in {k: v for k, v in fields.items() if k in allowed})
    plain_sql = f"UPDATE print_job SET {sets} WHERE id = ?"  # noqa: S608
    plain_vals = [v for k, v in fields.items() if k in allowed] + [row_id]
    with get_conn() as c:
        c.execute(plain_sql, plain_vals)
changes.note: print_job lacks an updated_at column. _build_update_sql always appends ', updated_at = datetime(now)' so we need a separate simpler form here, or add updated_at to print_job (recommended as part of a future schema migration).

verification: Log a print job, then edit it to change the filament grams
Verify filament inventory is adjusted by the delta, not the full new amount
Verify the print job row reflects the new values

tasks.title: Fix run.bat to use uv
tasks.rationale: Windows launcher bypasses uv and uses pip directly, producing non-reproducible installs that ignore uv.lock.

changes.new_content:
@echo off
REM Launcher for Accessibility Materials Project Manager — Windows
cd /d "%~dp0"

where uv >nul 2>&1
if errorlevel 1 (
    echo ERROR: 'uv' not found. Install it from https://docs.astral.sh/uv/
    pause
    exit /b 1
)

echo Starting Accessibility Materials Project Manager...
uv run AccessMan
pause

verification: run.bat launches the app via uv on Windows

tasks.title: Style fixes: docstring, future annotations, type hints, exception, inline imports
tasks.rationale: STYLE.md mandates all of these. Grouping them as a single low-risk cleanup pass.

changes.insert_at_top:
"""
Accessibility Materials Project Manager — TUI application entry point.

Built with Textual (https://textual.textualize.io). Manages inventory and
job-tracking for braille transcription and 3-D printing production workflows.
"""

changes.description: Add '-> None', '-> int | None', '-> ComposeResult' return types to all public Panel and Modal methods. Specifically: compose() -> ComposeResult, on_mount() -> None, refresh_table() -> None, _current_id() -> int | None, add() -> None, edit() -> None, delete() -> None, open_folder() -> None, save() -> None, cancel() -> None, confirm() -> None, close() -> None, handle_nav() -> None, action_refresh() -> None, _show_section() -> None.

changes.old: except (ValueError, Exception) as e:
changes.new: except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001 — intentional broad catch for user-facing error display

changes.description: Move 'import subprocess, os' and 'import sqlite3' from inside function bodies to the top-level import block, following STYLE.md import ordering: stdlib first.

tasks.title: Add Printer management UI (consistent with H-01 extensibility goal)
tasks.rationale: Printer names are hard-coded as seed data with no UI to add or rename printers. This is inconsistent with the dynamic materials system added in H-01.

changes.code:
def add_printer(name: str) -> int:
    """Insert a new printer; returns the new row id."""
    with get_conn() as c:
        cur = c.execute("INSERT INTO printer (name) VALUES (?)", (name,))
        return cur.lastrowid

def delete_printer(row_id: int) -> None:
    """Delete a printer record. Raises IntegrityError if print jobs reference it."""
    with get_conn() as c:
        c.execute("DELETE FROM printer WHERE id=?", (row_id,))

def update_printer(row_id: int, name: str) -> None:
    """Rename a printer."""
    with get_conn() as c:
        c.execute("UPDATE printer SET name=? WHERE id=?", (name, row_id))

tasks.title: Add changelog entry for released version 2026.5.6 in RELEASING.md
tasks.rationale: pyproject.toml is at version 2026.5.6 but RELEASING.md has no entry for it, only [Unreleased] and a TBD [0.1.0]. This violates the project's own release process.

changes.old:
## [0.1.0] — TBD

*Initial release.*
changes.new:
## [2026.5.6] — 2026-05-06

### Added
- Initial project scaffold: TUI application with six inventory/workflow panels
- SQLite database with tables for filament, braille paper, electronics, printers, print jobs, braille jobs, and LP/eBraille jobs
- BambuLabs P1S and Sovol SV08 Max pre-seeded as printer options
- Automatic filament gram deduction when a print job is logged
- Print file indexing: attached .3mf/.stl files copied to prints_files/ and recorded in the database
- Progress tracking with visual bar (███░░ 3/5) for braille and LP/eBraille jobs
- run.sh (Linux/macOS) and run.bat (Windows) launcher scripts
- pyproject.toml configured for uv, ruff, mypy, and pytest
- Console script entry point: uv run AccessMan
- Dynamic material categories and workflow steps tables for user-extensible options
- Admin panel for managing material categories, workflow steps, and printers

verification: RELEASING.md contains a dated [2026.5.6] section

tasks.title: Deduplicate dev dependencies in pyproject.toml
tasks.rationale: Dev deps are declared in both [project.optional-dependencies] and [tool.uv], creating a maintenance hazard.

changes.note: Remove the entire [project.optional-dependencies] block. The [tool.uv] dev-dependencies section is the canonical source used by uv. Add a comment above [tool.uv] explaining this.
changes.comment_to_add:
# Dev dependencies are managed here (uv-canonical).
# Do not duplicate in [project.optional-dependencies].

verification: uv sync --dev succeeds
uv run pytest succeeds
grep -A5 'optional-dependencies' pyproject.toml returns no results
